summary: Build a UK-focused daily check-in app for adults 65+ to track health status and provide early detection of fall risk.
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- Warm, reassuring app logo mark with simple symbol (steady-walk motif), flat design (uk-fallcheck-logo.dim_512x512.png)
- Minimal hero illustration of an older adult doing a daily check-in on a tablet, clean flat style, high contrast (checkin-hero.dim_1600x900.png)
- Set of 3 simple monochrome icons (dizzy, unsteady, sleep) in consistent stroke style (risk-icons-set.dim_1024x1024.png)

existing_features.title: Implement an onboarding flow for older adults (65+) that captures a minimal user profile for fall-risk context: age (must be 65+), sex (optional), height/weight (optional), mobility aids used (e.g., cane/walker), history of falls in last 12 months (yes/no + count), key conditions (e.g., dizziness, arthritis, Parkinson’s—checkboxes), and medications that may affect balance (simple yes/no). Store this profile per authenticated Internet Identity user.

Create a daily check-in form (one submission per calendar day per user, using UK local date) that collects health and fall-related signals such as: dizziness/lightheadedness, unsteadiness, new pain, fatigue/weakness, vision issues, medication changes today, alcohol intake, sleep quality, and whether the user had a near-fall or fall since last check-in. Persist each check-in entry to the backend with timestamp and UK date key.

Implement a fall-risk assessment computed from the latest check-in (and optionally recent history like last 7 days) that outputs: a numeric risk score (0–100), a risk level (Low/Medium/High), and a short set of plain-English contributing factors (e.g., “Reported dizziness today”, “Near-fall reported”). The scoring must be deterministic, transparent (rules-based), and configurable in backend constants.

Show tailored, non-alarming guidance based on the risk level, written for UK users in English (e.g., “Consider contacting your GP or NHS 111 for advice” for higher risk) and include a clear disclaimer that the app is not a medical device and does not provide a diagnosis or emergency response. Avoid claiming to predict falls with certainty.

Add a history view that lets users review past check-ins and risk levels over time (e.g., last 30 entries) with accessible, high-contrast charts or list summaries and the ability to open a specific day’s details.

Design the UI specifically for older adults: large default typography, high contrast, clear touch targets, simple language, and minimal steps. Include an accessibility settings panel with at least: text size (normal/large/largest) and contrast mode toggle, persisted locally in the browser.

Create a coherent visual theme for the app (colors, typography, spacing, components) suitable for a UK health check-in product and apply it consistently across onboarding, check-in, results, and history. Do not use a blue & purple palette.

Add basic input validation and friendly error handling: required fields in daily check-in must be validated client-side; backend must validate day uniqueness and reject invalid submissions; UI must present errors in plain English without exposing raw exceptions.

Add static informational pages: “About”, “How it works (rules-based risk)”, and “Privacy” explaining what data is stored in the canister, that no third-party services are used, and that the app is UK-focused. All text must be in English.

Add generated static images to support branding and usability, and render them in the UI (e.g., on the landing/onboarding screens). Store them as static assets under frontend/public/assets/generated and do not serve images through the backend.

new_feature_requests.summary: Daily check-in flow for elderly users (65+) to report health status
Fall-risk early detection feature based on check-in data (UK elderly focus)

Backend data model and API: implement Motoko types and stable storage for user profile and check-ins, with query/update methods to (1) get/set profile, (2) create daily check-in, (3) list check-ins, (4) get computed risk for a check-in. Ensure data is isolated per authenticated principal.

projectName: UK elderly daily health check-in with fall-risk early detection
